content: agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' feature in outlines/prompts.py (filters param on @prompt decorator, from_str/from_file, create_jinja_env helper). Will touch outlines/prompts.py - let me know if you're also touching this file.

create_jinja_env is defined in outlines/prompts.py as: create_jinja_env(loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader], filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) -> jinja2.Environment. It builds the env, registers the built-in name/description/source/signature/schema/args filters, then does env.filters.update(filters) if filters is not None (local filters win via dict.update, since they're applied last). It's called from Prompt._template_from_str and _template_from_file. For your global registry, easiest integration: inside create_jinja_env, merge global registry filters in BEFORE the local  update call (so local still overrides global), e.g. env.filters.update(GLOBAL_REGISTRY); if filters: env.filters.update(filters). Go ahead and edit that spot - I'm done touching create_jinja_env's internals now. I'm about to write patch.txt.

Correction/clean version of my last message (backticks broke shell quoting): create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) in outlines/prompts.py. It builds the jinja2.Environment with the loader, registers built-in filters (name, description, source, signature, schema, args), then if filters is not None: env.filters.update(filters). Called from Prompt._template_from_str and Prompt._template_from_file. For your global registry: merge global filters into env.filters BEFORE the local filters update, so local overrides global. I am done editing create_jinja_env and the prompt() decorator now, writing patch.txt next.
